mission.title: Install and Configure MicroK8s
mission.description: MicroK8s is a lightweight, single-package Kubernetes distribution designed for simplicity and speed, ideal for development, IoT, and edge computing. It provides a fully conformant Kubernetes experience with minimal overhead, making it perfect for local development environments. Key trade-offs include limited scalability compared to full Kubernetes clusters and reliance on the Snap package manager.

steps.title: Install MicroK8s
steps.description:
This step installs MicroK8s using Snap, which is the recommended method for installation.

```bash

sudo snap install microk8s --classic

```

> Installs the latest stable version of MicroK8s.

steps.title: Add User to MicroK8s Group
steps.description:
This step allows your user to run MicroK8s commands without sudo by adding it to the microk8s group.

```bash

sudo usermod -a -G microk8s $USER

```

> Replace $USER with your username if not using the current user.

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description:
This step checks if MicroK8s is running correctly by listing the nodes.

```bash

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> Use 'sudo' if you haven't added your user to the microk8s group yet.

steps.title: Enable Add-ons
steps.description:
This step enables essential add-ons like DNS and the Kubernetes dashboard for better functionality.

```bash

microk8s enable dns

microk8s enable dashboard

```

> Enables DNS for service discovery.
> Enables the Kubernetes dashboard for UI access.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MicroK8s
uninstall.description:
This step removes MicroK8s from your system. Be aware that this will delete all Kubernetes resources and configurations.

```bash

sudo snap remove microk8s

```

uninstall.title: Remove User from MicroK8s Group
uninstall.description:
This step removes your user from the microk8s group to clean up permissions.

```bash

sudo gpasswd -d $USER microk8s

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade MicroK8s
upgrade.description:
Ensure you back up your configurations before upgrading. This step upgrades MicroK8s to the latest version.

```bash

sudo snap refresh microk8s

microk8s status

```

> This command updates MicroK8s to the latest version.
> Verify that MicroK8s is running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'microk8s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s service may not be started.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo microk8s start' to start the MicroK8s service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s kubectl is not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Use 'microk8s kubectl' instead of 'kubectl' or add an alias in your shell configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubeconfig may not be set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'microk8s kubectl config view --raw > $HOME/.kube/config' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to enable addon: dns'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The DNS add-on may already be enabled or there may be a conflict.

**Fix:**
Check the status with 'microk8s status' and disable/re-enable DNS if necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.31 features and improved add-on management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for upgrading from v1.30.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'microk8s kubectl get nodes' showing your node in a Ready state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum resource requirements and has Snap installed.
